content: Just as language educators explicitly teach the metacognitive practice of written reflection to help high school students refine their thinking and correct misunderstandings, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be restructured to adopt the structured, scaffolded reflection pedagogy used in high school literacy curricula to improve their problem-solving consistency. This structured, skill-building approach to reflection differs from most current LLM self-reflection designs that treat reflection as an emergent, unstructured self-check rather than a teachable, scaffolded cognitive skill.
